feed.title: US.gov
feed.subtitle: Combined U.S. federal government feed: NSA, Department of War (Featured Stories, News, Advisories, News Releases), Space Force, FBI, NOAA, the USAGov and GSA blogs, GSA news releases, and U.S. Army news.

entry.title: DOW Awards a Nearly $7 Billion Oracle Agreement to Accelerate the Arsenal of Freedom

entry.dc:creator: War: News Releases

entry.title: Maintainers Work Quietly Behind Scenes Ensuring Global Power
entry.content: Crew chiefs assigned to the Air National Guard's 171st Air Refueling Wing, based near Pittsburgh, keep two aerial refueling flying squadrons of KC-135 Stratotanker refueling tanker aircraft mission ready.
entry.dc:creator: War: Featured Stories

entry.title: Navy Nurse Awarded for Pediatric Trauma Care Research, Improvements
entry.content: Navy Nurse Awarded for Pediatric Trauma Care Research, Improvements

entry.title: A UH-60 approaches a casualty pickup point during a joint medical evacuation training event with U.S. Army Soldiers from the 709th Medical Company (Area Support), Illinois Army National and members of the Malaysian army's 5th Infantry Division.
entry.content: A UH-60 approaches a casualty pickup point during a joint medical evacuation training event with U.S. Army Soldiers from the 709th Medical Company (Area Support), Illinois Army National and members of the Malaysian army's 5th Infantry Division.

entry.title: Every Second Matters
entry.content: Every Second Matters
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Hegseth, Caine Testify in Support of Supplemental Budget Request
entry.content: Secretary of War Pet Hegseth and Air Force Gen. Dan Caine, chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, appeared before the Senate Appropriations Committee on Capitol Hill, where the two leaders spoke in support of President Donald J. Trump's supplemental budget request to Congress.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: Large-Scale Cybersecurity Exercise Focuses on Operational Technology
entry.content: This year's iteration of the War Department's longest-running unclassified cyber defense exercise, Cyber Shield 2026, is largely focused on operational technology, military cyber professionals said during a virtual media roundtable.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: Signal Operations During Operation Bold Eagle
entry.content: Signal Operations During Operation Bold Eagle
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Basic Cadet Training Prepares New Cadets for Academy Life
entry.content: Every summer, the U.S. Air Force Academy in Colorado Springs, Colo., introduces its newest basic cadets to military life through basic cadet training.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: Army Contracting Support Enables Marine Operations Along Southern Border
entry.content: Army contracting support is essential for the Marine Corps' mission at the U.S. southern border. By walking the ground alongside the warfighters, the contracting team is better positioned to deliver the right capabilities, at the right time, in the right place.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: New York Guardsmen, Greenland Airports Partner to Improve Lodging Facility
entry.content: The New York Air National Guard, New York Army National Guard and Greenland Airports are working together to improve living conditions for airmen by repairing a long-standing roof issue at a lodging facility in Kangerlussuaq, Greenland.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: Army Reserve Soldiers from 319th Expeditionary Signal Battalion return home after hostile CENTCOM deployment

entry.title: DOW Identifies an Army Casualty
entry.content: The War Department announced the death of an active-duty soldier who was supporting overseas operations in Iraq.

entry.title: Air Force Reserve expands international relationships at GASCC, RIAT 2026
entry.content: Air Force Reserve senior leaders participated in bilateral engagements at the 2026 Global Air & Space Chiefs' Conference and RIAT, signing ToRs with allied nations to formalize cooperation through the Reserve Allies & Partners Program.
entry.dc:creator: Space Force

entry.title: Minnesota National Guard, Croatia Celebrate 30 Years of Partnership
entry.content: Minnesota and Croatia celebrated the 30th anniversary of their partnership through the National Guard Bureau's State Partnership Program during Exercise Combat Power 26, near Slunj, Croatia.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: Coast Guard Monitors Chinese Vessels in the Arctic
entry.content: The Coast Guard recently monitored two Chinese research ships as they transited north through the U.S. exclusive economic zone and over the U.S. extended continental shelf in the Bering Sea.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: Department of the Air Force appoints 14 members to Scientific Advisory Board
entry.content: These appointments, nominated by the secretary of the Air Force and approved by the secretary of War, bring a diverse range of expertise from the private sector and academia.
entry.dc:creator: Space Force

entry.title: [QK]NEX Sigonella Back-to-School Water Bottle Giveaway

entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Medal of Honor Monday: Army 2nd Lt. Robert Craig
entry.content: Army 2nd Lt. Robert Craig was an infantryman during World War II. He posthumously received the Medal of Honor for his heroic actions in Sicily during Operation Husky in 1943.
entry.dc:creator: War: Featured Stories

entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: DOW Identifies Army Casualties
entry.content: The War Department announced the death of two active-duty soldiers who were supporting Operation Inherent Resolve.

entry.title: Readout of the 45th U.S.-Jordan Joint Military Commission
entry.content: Assistant Secretary of War for International Security Affairs Daniel Zimmerman and Jordan's Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff Major General Yousef Al Hnaity co-chaired the 45th U.S.-Jordan Joint Military Commission in Amman, Jordan.
entry.dc:creator: War: News Releases

entry.title: Department of War Invests $7.1M to Enhance Space Solar Cell Coverglass Manufacturing
entry.content: The Department of War announced a $7.1 million investment to establish and scale domestic supply of space solar cell coverglass manufacturing in Twinsburg, Ohio.
entry.dc:creator: War: News Releases

entry.title: U.S., Canadian, Thai Navies Sharpen Maritime Capabilities During Exercise CARAT Thailand
entry.content: The United States, Thailand and Canada successfully concluded the Cooperation Afloat Readiness and Training Thailand maritime exercise in Sattahip, enhancing combined naval capabilities and reinforcing a shared commitment to a free and secure Pacific region.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: Medal of Honor Recipient Welcomes Newest Student Naval Aviators
entry.content: Retired Navy Capt. E. Royce Williams, the oldest living Medal of Honor recipient, returned to the base where he earned his gold wings, joining Naval Aviation Schools Command in congratulating 29 student naval aviators who completed their introductory flight evaluation training.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: First Guardians in part-time work roles join the Space Force
entry.content: The Space Force integrated 18 space-experienced Air Force Reservists into part-time Guardian roles, marking the first time Guardians will serve on non-sustained duty as part of a new single-component service structure.
entry.dc:creator: Space Force: Category 23812

entry.title: West Coast Gray Whale Population Increases
entry.content: West Coast Gray Whale Population Increases Gray whales were nearly hunted to extinction by commercial whaling. Protections under the MMPA, ESA, and the end of commercial whaling have allowed the species to recover. (Image credit: NOAA Fisheries (Permit #19091).) Download Image July 17, 2026 0 Off
entry.dc:creator: NOAA

entry.title: Humpback Whale Freed in Alaska
entry.content: Humpback Whale Freed in Alaska The NOAA Fisheries response team uses poles with specialized knives to cut entangling lines. (Image credit: Alaska Department of Fish and Game/Jamie Musbach (NOAA Fisheries Permit # 24359)) Download Image July 17, 2026 Fisheries whales 0 Off
entry.dc:creator: NOAA

entry.title: CSAF, CMSAF visit RAF Mildenhall
entry.content: Chief of Staff Gen. Ken Wilsbach and Chief Master Sgt. of the Air Force David Wolfe visited RAF Mildenhall to engage with Airmen and emphasize the importance of readiness and innovation.
entry.dc:creator: Space Force

entry.title: Space Force releases S5-S7 promotions
entry.content: The USSF selected 843 Guardians for promotion to the ranks of sergeant, technical sergeant, and master sergeant during the USSF 2026 Mega Board.
entry.dc:creator: Space Force: Category 23812

entry.title: Air Force releases field grade officer promotions
entry.content: The Air Force Personnel Center announced the selection of 2,447 active-duty officers for promotion to major during the P0426A Central Selection Board.
entry.dc:creator: Space Force

entry.title: AFN Europe Report - July 17, 2026
entry.content: AFN Europe Report - July 17, 2026
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Lights, camera, seafood! Bertha Fontaine and the Fisheries Test Kitchen
entry.content: Lights, camera, seafood! Bertha Fontaine and the Fisheries Test Kitchen Fontaine (center) frequently demonstrated seafood recipes on television programs. (Image credit: NOAA Magazine, Volume 2 Number 3, July 1972) Download Image July 20, 2026 As the U.S. government expanded its effort in the 1950s and 1960s to promote American seafood, the need for charismatic spokespeople became clear — people like Bertha Fontaine. The scene: Boxy television cameras point toward a brightly lit table. Plates of
entry.dc:creator: NOAA

entry.title: Signers of the Declaration of Independence: Delaware
entry.content: The 56 delegates who signed the Declaration of Independence are all considered Founding Fathers; three — Thomas McKean, George Read and Caesar Rodney — represented Delaware.
entry.dc:creator: War: Featured Stories

entry.title: Secretary of War General Officer Announcements for July 16, 2026
entry.content: Secretary of War Pete Hegseth announced President Donald J. Trump made general officer nominations.
entry.dc:creator: War: News Releases

entry.title: Secretary of War Flag Officer Announcements for July 16, 2026
entry.content: Secretary of War Pete Hegseth announced that President Donald J. Trump made flag officer nominations.
entry.dc:creator: War: News Releases

entry.title: Wyoming Air Guardsmen Activate for Wildland Firefighting in Western U.S.
entry.content: The Wyoming Air National Guard's 153rd Airlift Wing was activated to support wildland firefighting operations in the western United States, with significant fire activity in the Southwest, Great Basin, Rocky Mountain and Northwest geographic areas.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: AI-Powered Chatbot Helps Streamline Recruiting
entry.content: To help Army recruiters get information fast, enabling them to do their jobs at speed and with more authoritative resources, the Army developed Staff Sgt. Star, an artificial intelligence-powered chatbot.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: DHA Official Says TRICARE Pharmacy Ensuring Essential Medication Delivery Worldwide
entry.content: The War Department delivers one of the most comprehensive health benefits, through TRICARE, including an exceptional prescription drug benefit program, said David J. Smith, Defense Health Agency deputy director, during a hearing in Washington.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: Trump Announces $10B Investment in Defense Industry
entry.content: President Donald J. Trump announced that the War Department will be investing roughly $10 billion in materiel production while in support of more than 4,000 defense industry jobs across Pennsylvania.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: B-Roll: Canadian CP-140 Aurora flies during RIMPAC 2026
entry.content: B-Roll: Canadian CP-140 Aurora flies during RIMPAC 2026
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: U.S. Successfully Completes New Strikes in Iran
entry.content: U.S. Successfully Completes New Strikes in Iran
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Images - Page 2 - Tag honor
entry.content: Images - Page 2 - Tag honor
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Statement by Chief Pentagon Spokesman, Sean Parnell, on Enhanced Screening Protocol to Optimize Warfighter Performance and Enhance Force Readiness
entry.content: Secretary of War Pete Hegseth directed an enhanced testosterone deficiency screening protocol for service members to optimize performance and maximize mission readiness.
entry.dc:creator: War: News Releases

entry.title: U.S. Forces Continue Providing Disaster Relief Assistance in Venezuela
entry.content: U.S. armed forces are continuing to provide disaster relief assistance to the people of Venezuela in the aftermath of two devastating June 24 earthquakes.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: Declassification of the Department of War Internal After-Action Review of the Coronavirus Disease 2019 Vaccine Mandate Report
entry.content: Secretary of War Pete Hegseth directed the National Academy of Public Administration to conduct an after-action review capturing lessons learned to improve future policy.
entry.dc:creator: War: News Releases

entry.title: Air Force conducts live-fire test for Collaborative Combat Aircraft program
entry.content: The Department of the Air Force successfully conducted a live-fire test of a YFQ-44A Collaborative Combat Aircraft, firing an AIM-120 weapon at a digital target. The test demonstrates program maturity and operational validation while emphasizing the retention of human oversight for weapon release.
entry.dc:creator: Space Force

entry.title: CSAF, CMSAF discuss readiness, quality of life during Aviano AB visit
entry.content: CSAF Wilsbach and CMSAF Wolfe visited Aviano Air Base to discuss readiness and quality-of-life initiatives with Airmen of the 31st Fighter Wing.
entry.dc:creator: Space Force

entry.title: Department of War Announces Landmark Agreements With New Entrants to Accelerate Low-Cost, Air-Launched Cruise Missiles
entry.content: The Department of War announced framework agreements with three industry partners to complete testing and qualification of the Family of Affordable Mass Missiles Air Force program.
entry.dc:creator: War: News Releases

entry.title: Special Event Security, Part Two: How the FBI Keeps the Public Safe at Gatherings
entry.content: The Bureau plays a critical role in keeping everyone safe at public gatherings defined as special events by the U.S. Department of Homeland Security. Qualifying events can be almost any kind of public gathering: parades, local fairs, music festivals, foot races, concerts, awards shows, and sporting events, just to name a few.
entry.dc:creator: FBI: Stories

entry.title: Images - Tag MP
entry.content: Images - Tag MP
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Hegseth Hosts Iraqi Prime Minister at Pentagon
entry.content: Secretary of War Pete Hegseth welcomed Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi to the Pentagon, where the two leaders met to discuss the Global Coalition to Defeat ISIS, the end of the Operation Inherent Resolve mission and the future of the U.S.-Iraq relationship.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: DHA Partners With Marines to Refine Portable TBI Assessment Technology for Austere Battlefield
entry.content: Team members assigned to the Defense Health Agency's Warfighter Readiness, Performance and Brain Health initiative hosted an end user touchpoint at Camp Lejeune, N.C., to assess new capabilities for evaluating traumatic brain injuries on the battlefield.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: The Department of War's Office of Strategic Capital Introduces National Security Fund Finance Program
entry.content: The Department of War's Office of Strategic Capital announced the introduction of the National Security Fund Finance program, which aims to provide capital support to credit funds addressing shortages, gaps and vulnerabilities in critical minerals vital to U.S. national security.
entry.dc:creator: War: News Releases

entry.title: Readout of Secretary of War Pete Hegseth's Meeting With Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi
entry.content: Secretary of War Pete Hegseth met with Iraq's Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi at the Pentagon to discuss the global coalition to defeat ISIS, the end of the Operation Inherent Resolve mission from Iraq and the future of the U.S.-Iraq relationship.
entry.dc:creator: War: News Releases

entry.title: Self-Propelled Artillery Systems Mechanic 91P
entry.content: Self-Propelled Artillery Systems Mechanic 91P
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Network Communication Systems Specialist 25H - :15
entry.content: Network Communication Systems Specialist 25H - :15
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: D.C. National Guard Boosts Homeland Defense With First Maritime Security Vessel
entry.content: The District of Columbia National Guard's 260th Special Purpose Brigade commissioned its first maritime security vessel, boosting homeland defense and interagency coordination to protect the capital's waterways and critical infrastructure.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: BJACH Demonstrates the TRICARE Q-Anywhere Mobile App
entry.content: BJACH Demonstrates the TRICARE Q-Anywhere Mobile App
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: 21st Theater Sustainment Command Recovers Drone Components to Support Innovation
entry.content: 21st Theater Sustainment Command Recovers Drone Components to Support Innovation
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Radio News 260715-260717
entry.content: Radio News 260715-260717
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Self-Propelled Artillery Systems Mechanic 91P - :15

entry.title: What
entry.content: What
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Best Warrior Competition 2026 Ruck March
entry.content: Best Warrior Competition 2026 Ruck March
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Guardian astronaut brings operational medicine experience to ISS mission
entry.content: U.S. Space Force Col. Anil Menon launched aboard NASA’s Expedition 75 mission to the International Space Station, bringing with him a career shaped by operational medicine, military service and human spaceflight support.
entry.dc:creator: Space Force: Category 23812

entry.title: Skyfall Simulation: NATO Partners Rehearse Downed Aircraft Response
entry.content: Kosovo Force Regional Command East's Task Force Aviation and multinational partners conducted a downed aircraft exercise near Camp Bondsteel, Kosovo, rehearsing search and rescue, medical evacuation and aircraft recovery procedures following a simulated UH-60 Black Hawk helicopter hard landing.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: Department of War Issues Final FY26 APFIT Awards, Surpassing $2 Billion Milestone and Debuting Software Capabilities
entry.content: The War Department announced a new round of awards under the Accelerate the Procurement and Fielding of Innovative Technologies program, marking a major milestone as the program surpasses $2 billion in total awards since its inception.
entry.dc:creator: War: News Releases

entry.title: Massachusetts Man Convicted of Violating U.S. Sanctions Against Iran
entry.content: A Massachusetts man was convicted in federal court in Boston following a 14-day jury trial for charges related to a scheme to illegally export sophisticated electronic components from the United States to Iran.
entry.dc:creator: FBI: Press Releases

entry.title: Images - Page 26 - Tag rifles
entry.content: Images - Page 26 - Tag rifles
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: 13th MEU Conducts Integrated Training while Underway with the MKIARG
entry.content: 13th MEU Conducts Integrated Training while Underway with the MKIARG
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: USAMMC-E welcomes new commander
entry.content: USAMMC-E welcomes new commander
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: GSA Solicits Industry Feedback to Enhance American-Made Product Procurement
entry.content: GSA Solicits Industry Feedback to Enhance American-Made Product Procurement
entry.dc:creator: GSA News Releases

entry.title: War Department Changes Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification Requirements
entry.content: The War Department announced the suspension of the Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification phase two requirements, initially scheduled to take effect in November, and launched a comprehensive review of the entire CMMC program.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: NSI Aboard Recruit Training Command
entry.content: NSI Aboard Recruit Training Command
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Lt. Col. Brandon Staub
entry.content: Lt. Col. Brandon Staub

entry.title: 75th Air Base Wing Change of Command

entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: CMMC Phase II Suspended to Boost DIB Innovation
entry.content: CMMC Phase II Suspended to Boost DIB Innovation
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: FBI New Orleans: B-Roll Baton Rouge Landfill Search
entry.content: FBI New Orleans: B-Roll Baton Rouge Landfill Search
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Space Force launches Initial Enlisted Bonus Pilot Program to maximize readiness, return on investment
entry.content: The United States Space Force has announced implementation of a temporary Initial Enlistment Bonus Pilot Program, offering a $25,000 bonus to incoming enlisted Guardians who commit to an eight-year active-duty contract.
entry.dc:creator: Space Force: Category 23812

entry.title: Forging the Arsenal of Freedom: Department of War Suspends CMMC Phase II Requirements
entry.content: The Department of War announced the immediate suspension of the Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification Phase II requirements, which were originally scheduled to come into effect on Nov. 10, 2026.
entry.dc:creator: War: News Releases

entry.title: 14 Guardians graduate inaugural Regional Space Advisor Academics Course
entry.content: The U.S. Space Force graduated 14 Guardians from its first Regional Space Advisor Academics Course at the Pentagon. The program aims to develop international affairs and security cooperation skills within the Space Force to strengthen global partnerships and support the National Defense Strategy.
entry.dc:creator: Space Force: Category 23812

entry.title: AMCOM town hall addresses workforce priorities, safety and transition
entry.content: AMCOM town hall addresses workforce priorities, safety and transition
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Kentucky Guard hosts K-REP Summit to strengthen disaster response partnerships
entry.content: Kentucky Guard hosts K-REP Summit to strengthen disaster response partnerships
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Protecting the living waters of the Channel Islands from marine debris
entry.content: Protecting the living waters of the Channel Islands from marine debris Visitors to Hendry's Beach in Santa Barbara enjoy a beautiful summer sunset walk with NOAA's Channel Islands National Marine Sanctuary off in the distance. (Image credit: Claire Fackler/NOAA) Download Image July 7, 2026 Channel Islands National Park and Channel Islands National Marine Sanctuary protect some of California’s richest coastal and marine habitats. Keeping these remote islands and surrounding waters free of marine
entry.dc:creator: NOAA

entry.title: Adopt a Drifter Program grows this summer
entry.content: Adopt a Drifter Program grows this summer Teacher at Sea Susan Dee deploys a drifter buoy. (Image credit: NOAA Teacher at Sea) Download Image June 26, 2026 More than 20 years after the first teacher adopted a drifter, NOAA’s Adopt a Drifter Program , co-led by the Global Ocean Monitoring and Observing Program (GOMO) and the Atlantic Oceanographic and Meteorological Laboratory (AOML), continues to grow and evolve this summer. Education 0 Off
entry.dc:creator: NOAA

entry.title: The story behind the shot: 2025 winners share tips before the next contest begins
entry.content: The story behind the shot: 2025 winners share tips before the next contest begins Stargazing near the Old Seven Mile Bridge, Florida Keys National Marine Sanctuary (Image credit: Daniel Eidsmoe) Download Image May 19, 2026 Each year, the Get Into Your Sanctuary Photo Contest invites photographers to capture the beauty, biodiversity, and recreational experiences found across national marine sanctuaries and beyond. From quiet intertidal moments along the Olympic Coast to swirling shoals of squid i
entry.dc:creator: NOAA

entry.title: CSAF Letter to the Force on Standards
entry.content: Chief of Staff Gen. Kenneth S. Wilsbach released a letter to Airmen on standards.
entry.dc:creator: Space Force

entry.title: Building community resilience through CALM
entry.content: Building community resilience through CALM Upper Lake Park bluff tour in 2025. (Image credit: Lydia Salus, Wisconsin Coastal Management Program) Download Image July 10, 2026 The Takeaway: State partners in Wisconsin joined to create the Collaborative Action for Lake Michigan (CALM) Coastal Resilience network to build resilience and mitigate hazards by increasing coordination among local, regional, and state agencies. Education 0 Off
entry.dc:creator: NOAA

entry.title: Florida partners supercharge teacher workshop at the Guana Tolomato Matanzas Reserve
entry.content: Florida partners supercharge teacher workshop at the Guana Tolomato Matanzas Reserve View of the transition between hammock, marsh and the Tolomato River at the Guana Tolomato Matanzas National Estuarine Research Reserve. (Image credit: Savanna Mathis) July 10, 2026 Environmental educators from different organizations, led by Guana Tolomato Matanzas National Estuarine Research Reserve, hosted a two-day Teachers on the Estuary (TOTE) workshop for Florida teachers to explore new curricula, resourc
entry.dc:creator: NOAA

entry.title: Minnesota, Texas Sea Grant programs to lead next aquaculture leadership academy
entry.content: Minnesota, Texas Sea Grant programs to lead next aquaculture leadership academy Sea Grant Aquaculture Leadership Academy participants tour a Sandbar Oyster Company oyster and salt marsh restoration project North Carolina. (Image credit: LaDon Swann) Download Image April 6, 2026 The leaders will include Fisheries and Aquaculture Extension Educator Julia Grenn and Extension Program Leader and Aquaculture Extension Educator Amy Schrank, both of Minnesota Sea Grant, and Aquaculture Specialist Mario
entry.dc:creator: NOAA

entry.title: City nature challenge at Mallows Bay
entry.content: City nature challenge at Mallows Bay Photos uploaded to iNaturalist help record biodiversity in the sanctuary. (Image credit: Taylor Schneider/NOAA) Download Image May 26, 2026 For the fourth year in a row, Mallows Bay-Potomac River National Marine Sanctuary participated in the worldwide City Nature Challenge that occurred from April 24 to 27, 2026. The City Nature Challenge is a friendly collaboration among cities all over the world to share observations of nature using the iNaturalist app. Par
entry.dc:creator: NOAA

entry.title: U.S. Sea Drones Strike Submarine and Ship Maintenance Facility in Iran
entry.content: U.S. Sea Drones Strike Submarine and Ship Maintenance Facility in Iran
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Professional pevelopment workshop brings deep-sea science to Palau’s middle and high school classrooms
entry.content: Professional pevelopment workshop brings deep-sea science to Palau’s middle and high school classrooms A shallow coral reef community in Palau National Marine Sanctuary. (Image credit: Manu San Felix.) Download Image May 20, 2026 From March 31 to April 1, the Palau International Coral Reef Center (PICRC), in collaboration with educators from NOAA Ocean Exploration and Palau’s Ministry of Education, successfully conducted a two-day professional development workshop for 27 school teachers from acr
entry.dc:creator: NOAA

entry.title: New educational resource kit brings Arctic Report Card to K-16 audiences
entry.content: New educational resource kit brings Arctic Report Card to K-16 audiences (Image credit: Alex Rivest) Download Image May 29, 2026 The Arctic Report Card Educational Resource Kit is a curated collection of resources for anyone interested in the Arctic Report Card (ARC), including educators and students, and designed to be used with all editions of the Arctic Report Card. This resource was created alongside the release of the 20th anniversary Arctic Report Card in December 2025 and the National Sci
entry.dc:creator: NOAA
